errors.meaning: The Lambda deployment package exceeds the AWS hard limit of 10MB compressed. Deployment will fail.
causes: Large dependency bundled instead of externalized (>1MB package not in excludeDependencies)
AWS SDK v2 (aws-sdk) included in bundle — adds ~50MB uncompressed
Multiple large dependencies not externalized
Source maps or test files included in production bundle

resolution.action: Identify large dependencies in the bundle
Add large dependencies (>1MB) to excludeDependencies in build config
Verify AWS SDK v3 clients are externalized
Rebuild Lambda bundle
Verify bundle size is under 10MB

errors.decisionRule: If bundle exceeds 10MB, externalize dependencies. Do NOT suggest splitting into multiple Lambdas. Do NOT suggest ignoring — AWS will reject the deployment.

errors.meaning: A required npm package is not installed or not resolvable during build.
causes: Package not listed in package.json dependencies
yarn install not run after adding dependency
Package name typo in import statement
Package externalized in build but not available in Lambda runtime

resolution.action: Verify the package name is correct (check for typos)
Install the package
Run yarn install to ensure lockfile is updated
If externalized, verify it is available in Lambda runtime or layer
Rebuild

errors.decisionRule: If a module is missing, install it. Do NOT suggest alternative packages unless the original is deprecated.

errors.meaning: TypeScript path aliases are not resolving correctly during build.
causes: tsconfig.json paths configuration is incorrect or missing
tsc-alias post-processing step not running
Path alias used for a file that does not exist
Build target missing path alias resolution plugin

resolution.action: Verify tsconfig.json has correct paths configuration
Verify the target file exists at the resolved path
Run yarn types to regenerate path mappings
Rebuild

errors.decisionRule: If path aliases fail, fix the configuration. Do NOT replace path aliases with relative imports — that violates architecture boundaries.

errors.meaning: TypeScript type checking found type errors in the source code.
causes: Incorrect type usage with framework types (wrong generic parameters)
Missing type imports
Incompatible TypeScript version (requires 5.8+)
Breaking change in framework types after upgrade

resolution.action: Read the full error message — it identifies the file, line, and expected types
Check the correct type signature in the runtime-api-core skill
Fix the type usage to match the expected signature
If error appeared after upgrade, consult the versioning-and-migration skill
Run yarn types to verify the fix

errors.decisionRule: If TypeScript reports a type error, fix the types. Do NOT use @ts-ignore, do NOT use 'as any' casts, do NOT disable strict mode.

errors.meaning: CDK cannot synthesize the CloudFormation template from the CDK app.
causes: CDK code imports runtime code from @core/* or @lib/* (architecture violation)
Missing CDK dependency (aws-cdk-lib or constructs)
Incorrect construct props (wrong types or missing required props)
Circular dependency between stacks

resolution.action: Verify src/infra/ does NOT import from @core/* or @lib/*
Verify aws-cdk-lib and constructs are in devDependencies
Check construct props match the expected interface
If circular dependency: extract shared resources into a separate stack
Rebuild CDK

errors.decisionRule: If CDK synthesis fails due to architecture violation, move the import to the correct layer. Do NOT add @core/* or @lib/* to infra code.

errors.meaning: The CloudFormation stack operation failed and rolled back.
causes: IAM permissions insufficient for the operation
Resource name conflict (resource already exists with same name)
Resource limit exceeded in AWS account
Dependent resource not yet available

resolution.action: Check CloudFormation Events tab for the specific resource that failed
Read the StatusReason for the failed resource
If IAM: Add required permissions to the deployment role
If name conflict: Use CDK-generated names (do NOT hardcode resource names)
Redeploy after fixing the root cause

errors.decisionRule: If CloudFormation rolls back, identify the SPECIFIC failed resource. Do NOT retry without fixing the cause. Do NOT delete the stack unless explicitly asked.

errors.pattern: RequestEntityTooLargeException|Unzipped size must be smaller than
errors.meaning: The Lambda deployment package exceeds AWS limits even after compression.
causes: Dependencies not properly externalized in build config
Source maps or test files included in deployment package
Native binaries included (e.g., sharp, canvas)

resolution.action: Review excludeDependencies in build config — add missing large packages
Verify .serverless/*.zip contents
Remove source maps from production builds
For native binaries: use Lambda layers instead
Rebuild and redeploy

errors.decisionRule: If the package is too large, externalize more dependencies or use Lambda layers. Do NOT suggest increasing Lambda limits (they are fixed by AWS).

errors.meaning: The Lambda execution role lacks permissions for an AWS service call.
causes: New AWS SDK call added without corresponding IAM policy
IAM policy not attached to the Lambda execution role
Resource ARN incorrect in policy statement
Cross-account access without proper trust policy

resolution.action: Identify the exact AWS action from the error message
Add the permission via CDK L2 grant methods
Redeploy the infrastructure stack
Verify the Lambda can now perform the operation

errors.decisionRule: If IAM permission is missing, add it via CDK L2 grant methods. Do NOT use wildcard (*) permissions. Do NOT attach AdministratorAccess.

errors.pattern: No matching bindings found for serviceIdentifier|Service not registered
errors.meaning: A ties class was declared but the framework could not instantiate it.
causes: Ties class does not have a proper constructor
Ties class has unresolvable constructor dependencies
Ties class was not registered with the container (SSR handlers using Inversify)
Circular dependency between ties classes

resolution.action: Verify the ties class has a parameterless constructor or all dependencies are resolvable
For microservice pattern: Ensure the class is a plain class with a constructor
For SSR pattern: Verify the class is registered in runtime.container.ts
Check for circular dependencies between service classes
Test locally

errors.decisionRule: If a service is missing from the container, register it or fix its constructor. Do NOT catch and ignore the error. Do NOT provide a fallback value.

errors.pattern: Duplicate lambda id|Lambda with id '.*' already registered
errors.meaning: Two or more LambdaDefinition entries within the same microservice have the same id value.
causes: Copy-paste error when creating new Lambda definitions
Two handlers accidentally given the same id string
Auto-generated IDs colliding (rare)

resolution.action: Search the microservice definition for duplicate id values
Rename one of the duplicates to a unique identifier
Ensure each id is descriptive and unique within the microservice
resolution.code:
{ id: 'get-users' }    // Unique, descriptive
{ id: 'create-user' }  // Unique, descriptive

resolution.action: Rebuild and redeploy

errors.decisionRule: If Lambda IDs conflict, rename one. Do NOT remove the id field — it is used for routing and identification.

errors.pattern: Handler is not a function|event\.ties is undefined
errors.meaning: The handler function signature does not match what the framework expects.
causes: Using factory-style handler with object-based ties (or vice versa)
Handler not returning the expected response shape for the integration type
Missing async keyword on handler function
Handler accessing event.ties but ties not properly configured

resolution.action: Verify handler signature matches the object-based pattern
Verify the handler is async or returns a Promise
Verify ties property uses object syntax with class constructors
Verify the response shape matches the integration type

errors.decisionRule: If the handler type is wrong, fix the handler signature. Do NOT wrap it in a try-catch that returns a generic response.

errors.meaning: The integration-specific configuration is missing required fields or has invalid values.
causes: Missing required field (e.g., method for HTTP, queue for SQS)
Invalid value for a field (e.g., unsupported HTTP method)
Wrong integration property name (e.g., httpConfig instead of http)
Multiple integration configs on one Lambda (only one is allowed)

resolution.action: Check the exact integration config interface in the runtime-api-integrations skill
Verify all required fields are present with valid values
Verify only ONE integration config property is set per Lambda definition
Fix the configuration to match the interface

errors.decisionRule: If integration config is invalid, fix it to match the exact interface. Do NOT invent fields. Consult the integration configs reference.

errors.pattern: Type '.*' is not assignable to type 'LambdaDefinition<
errors.meaning: The generic type parameters on LambdaDefinition do not match the actual types used.
causes: TTies type does not match the object keys in ties property
TSnapshot type does not match the return type of init function
TIntegration does not match the integration config property used

resolution.action: Define the ties type to match the ties property exactly
If using init/snapshot, ensure TSnapshot matches init return type
If specifying integration kind, ensure it matches the config property used

errors.decisionRule: If generic parameters are wrong, fix the type definition. Do NOT use 'any' or 'unknown' to suppress the error.

errors.pattern: Type '.*' is not assignable to type 'TiesConstructors<
errors.meaning: The ties property expects class constructors but received instances or other values.
causes: Passing new Service() instead of Service (instance vs constructor)
Passing a factory function instead of a class constructor
Passing a plain object instead of a class

resolution.action: Pass the class itself, NOT an instance
resolution.code: ties: { userService: UsersService }  // Class constructor, not new UsersService()

resolution.action: Ensure the class has a constructor compatible with new (...args) => T
If using a factory, convert it to a class with a constructor

errors.decisionRule: If TiesConstructors fails, pass class constructors. The framework handles instantiation. Do NOT instantiate services manually.

errors.pattern: Cannot find module '@worktif/runtime'|has no exported member
errors.meaning: The import path is wrong or the exported member does not exist in the public API.
causes: Wrong import path (deep import instead of package root)
Member was renamed or removed in a framework update
Package not installed
Using internal types not exported from the public API

resolution.action: Use the correct import paths
resolution.code:
import { MicroserviceDefinition, LambdaDefinition } from '@worktif/runtime';
import { RuntimeInfraStack, BrowserProviderStack, RuntimeWebStack } from '@worktif/runtime/infra';

resolution.action: If member not found: Check the framework version and consult migration guide
If package not installed: yarn add @worktif/runtime
Do NOT import from internal paths like @worktif/runtime/src/lib/...

errors.decisionRule: If an import fails, use the documented public API paths. Do NOT reach into internal module paths.

errors.pattern: Property '.*' does not exist on type '.*Event'
errors.meaning: Accessing a property on the Lambda event that does not exist for the configured integration type.
causes: Accessing HTTP-specific properties (pathParameters, body) on a non-HTTP handler
Accessing SQS-specific properties (Records) on a non-SQS handler
Using wrong event type for the integration kind
Missing type annotation on handler parameter

resolution.action: Verify the integration type matches the event properties being accessed
Use the correct event type for the integration kind
Check the integration-event mapping in runtime-api-integrations skill

errors.decisionRule: If event properties are wrong, match them to the correct integration type. Do NOT cast the event to 'any'.
